# services.ncps

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.ncps`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.ncps.analytics.reporting.enable` | `boolean` | Enable reporting anonymous usage statistics (DB type, Lock type, Total Size) to the project maintainers. |
| `services.ncps.analytics.reporting.samples` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Enable printing the analytics samples to stdout. This is useful for debugging and verification purposes only.. |
| `services.ncps.cache.allowDeleteVerb` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Whether to allow the DELETE verb to delete narinfo and nar files from the cache. . |
| `services.ncps.cache.allowPutVerb` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Whether to allow the PUT verb to push narinfo and nar files directly to the cache. . |
| `services.ncps.cache.cdc.avg` | `32 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 4294967295 (both inclusive)` | The average chunk size for CDC in bytes. |
| `services.ncps.cache.cdc.enabled` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Whether to enable Content-Defined Chunking (CDC) for deduplication (experimental). . |
| `services.ncps.cache.cdc.max` | `32 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 4294967295 (both inclusive)` | The maximum chunk size for CDC in bytes. |
| `services.ncps.cache.cdc.min` | `32 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 4294967295 (both inclusive)` | The minimum chunk size for CDC in bytes. |
| `services.ncps.cache.database.pool.maxIdleConns` | `signed integer` | Maximum number of idle connections in the pool (0 = use database-specific defaults). |
| `services.ncps.cache.database.pool.maxOpenConns` | `signed integer` | Maximum number of open connections to the database (0 = use database-specific defaults). |
| `services.ncps.cache.databaseURL` | `null or string` | The URL of the database (currently only SQLite is supported) |
| `services.ncps.cache.databaseURLFile` | `null or absolute path` | File containing the URL of the database. |
| `services.ncps.cache.hostName` | `string` | The hostname of the cache server. **This is used to generate the private key used for signing store paths (.narinfo)** |
| `services.ncps.cache.lock.allowDegradedMode` | `boolean` | Allow falling back to local locks if Redis is unavailable (WARNING: breaks HA guarantees). |
| `services.ncps.cache.lock.backend` | `one of "local", "redis"` | Lock backend to use: 'local' (single instance), 'redis' (distributed). |
| `services.ncps.cache.lock.downloadTTL` | `string` | TTL for download locks (per-hash locks). |
| `services.ncps.cache.lock.lruTTL` | `string` | TTL for LRU lock (global exclusive lock). |
| `services.ncps.cache.lock.redisKeyPrefix` | `string` | Prefix for all Redis lock keys (only used when Redis is configured). |
| `services.ncps.cache.lock.retry.initialDelay` | `string` | Initial retry delay for distributed locks. |
| `services.ncps.cache.lock.retry.jitter` | `boolean` | Enable jitter in retry delays to prevent thundering herd. |
| `services.ncps.cache.lock.retry.maxAttempts` | `signed integer` | Maximum number of retry attempts for distributed locks. |
| `services.ncps.cache.lock.retry.maxDelay` | `string` | Maximum retry delay for distributed locks (exponential backoff caps at this). |
| `services.ncps.cache.lru.schedule` | `null or string` | The cron spec for cleaning the store to keep it under config.ncps.cache.maxSize. Refer to https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/robfig/cron/v3#hdr-Usage for documentation. |
| `services.ncps.cache.lru.scheduleTimeZone` | `string` | The name of the timezone to use for the cron schedule. See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones> for a comprehensive list of possible values for this setting. |
| `services.ncps.cache.maxSize` | `null or string` | The maximum size of the store. It can be given with units such as 5K, 10G etc. Supported units: B, K, M, G, T. |
| `services.ncps.cache.redis` | `null or (submodule)` | Configure Redis. |
| `services.ncps.cache.redis.addresses` | `list of string` | A list of host:port for the Redis servers that are part of a cluster. To use a single Redis instance, just set this to its single address. |
| `services.ncps.cache.redis.database` | `signed integer` | Redis database number (0-15) |
| `services.ncps.cache.redis.password` | `null or string` | Redis password for authentication (for Redis ACL). |
| `services.ncps.cache.redis.passwordFile` | `null or absolute path` | File containing the redis password for authentication (for Redis ACL). |
| `services.ncps.cache.redis.poolSize` | `signed integer` | Redis connection pool size. |
| `services.ncps.cache.redis.useTLS` | `boolean` | Use TLS for Redis connection. |
| `services.ncps.cache.redis.username` | `null or string` | Redis username for authentication (for Redis ACL). |
| `services.ncps.cache.secretKeyPath` | `null or absolute path` | The path to load the secretKey for signing narinfos. Leave this empty to automatically generate a private/public key. |
| `services.ncps.cache.signNarinfo` | `boolean` | Whether to sign narInfo files or passthru as-is from upstream |
| `services.ncps.cache.storage.local` | `absolute path` | The local directory for storing configuration and cached store paths. This is ignored if services.ncps.cache.storage.s3 is not null. |
| `services.ncps.cache.storage.s3` | `null or (submodule)` | Use S3 for storage instead of local storage. |
| `services.ncps.cache.storage.s3.accessKeyIdPath` | `absolute path` | The path to a file containing only the access-key-id. |
| `services.ncps.cache.storage.s3.bucket` | `string` | The name of the S3 bucket. |
| `services.ncps.cache.storage.s3.endpoint` | `string` | S3-compatible endpoint URL with scheme. |
| `services.ncps.cache.storage.s3.forcePathStyle` | `boolean` | Force path-style S3 addressing (bucket/key vs key.bucket). |
| `services.ncps.cache.storage.s3.region` | `null or string` | The S3 region. |
| `services.ncps.cache.storage.s3.secretAccessKeyPath` | `absolute path` | The path to a file containing only the secret-access-key. |
| `services.ncps.cache.tempPath` | `absolute path` | The path to the temporary directory that is used by the cache to download NAR files |
| `services.ncps.cache.upstream.dialerTimeout` | `null or string` | Timeout for establishing TCP connections to upstream caches (e.g., 3s, 5s, 10s). |
| `services.ncps.cache.upstream.publicKeys` | `list of string` | A list of public keys of upstream caches in the format `host[-[0-9]*]:public-key`. This flag is used to verify the signatures of store paths downloaded from upstream caches. |
| `services.ncps.cache.upstream.responseHeaderTimeout` | `null or string` | Timeout for waiting for upstream server's response headers. |
| `services.ncps.cache.upstream.urls` | `list of string` | A list of URLs of upstream binary caches. |
| `services.ncps.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable ncps: Nix binary cache proxy service implemented in Go. |
| `services.ncps.logLevel` | `one of "trace", "debug", "info", "warn", "error", "fatal", "panic"` | Set the level for logging. Refer to <https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/rs/zerolog#readme-leveled-logging> for more information. |
| `services.ncps.netrcFile` | `null or absolute path` | The path to netrc file for upstream authentication. When unspecified ncps will look for \`\`$HOME/.netrc`. | | `services.ncps.openTelemetry.enable`|`boolean`| Whether to enable Enable OpenTelemetry logs, metrics, and tracing. | |`services.ncps.openTelemetry.grpcURL`|`null or string`| Configure OpenTelemetry gRPC URL. Missing or "https" scheme enables secure gRPC, "insecure" otherwise. Omit to emit telemetry to stdout. | |`services.ncps.package`|`package`| The ncps package to use. | |`services.ncps.prometheus.enable`|`boolean`| Whether to enable Enable Prometheus metrics endpoint at /metrics. | |`services.ncps.server.addr`|`string\` | The address and port the server listens on. |
